title: Holcosorus bisulcatus
intro: Holcosorus bisulcatus is a type of vine plant, also known as the "Tongkat Ali vine", which is native to Southeast Asia and is a climber that uses other plants for support. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, which include improving cardiovascular health, reducing inflammation, and enhancing sexual function in males. The bioactive constituents of Holcosorus bisulcatus include compounds such as eurystatin, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Holcosorus bisulcatus is commonly prepared as a crude extract, powder, or capsule, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
taxonomy: Holcosorus bisulcatus is a genus of flowering plants in the family Celastraceae. It is native to tropical and subtropical regions of Australia, New Guinea, and nearby islands. The classification of Holcosorus bisulcatus includes being a member of the order Celastrales. Further classification places it within the clade of flowering plants, or angiosperms. This classification is based on its morphological characteristics and phylogenetic relationships.
